Tests the behavior of `Math.abs` with negative zero in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the `Math.abs` function in JavaScript. It verifies that the absolute value of negative zero (`-0.0`) is positive zero (`+0.0`). This test ensures that the `Math.abs` function correctly handles the edge case of negative zero, which is a special floating-point value in JavaScript. The assertion uses the `assert` function to confirm that the expression evaluates to `true`, indicating that the function behaves as expected.
